summary: Verified dynamic rank refactoring: all leaderboard paths now compute rank from position (no stored rank/rank_ts fields). Backend pytest: 6/6 passed, 1 skipped due to expected DB inconsistency (papers in rankings but not in papers collection — mentioned in problem statement). Frontend infinite scroll verified visually: ranks 1588-1601 displayed continuously across page boundary 1400/1600 with NO gap and NO restart at 1.
action_items: Consider adding data-testid attributes to rank cells in the leaderboard table for future automated testing; current ranks are bare <span> elements without testids.
Some rankings docs reference paper_ids missing from papers collection (preview DB partial data) — paper detail returns 404 for those. Not a refactor regression but worth a data integrity sweep.
critical_code_review_comments: _rank_doc_to_entry returning rank=0 as placeholder is fragile — if a future caller forgets to overwrite, users would see rank=0. Consider a guard or sentinel that raises if not overwritten before serialization.

Paper detail current_rank uses count_documents({ts_score: {$gt: X}}) + 1 — for papers tied at the same ts_score, all tied papers report the same rank (competition ranking). Leaderboard uses ts_score+paper_id tiebreak, so a paper's leaderboard position may differ from current_rank by up to N (where N is tie group size). Tests passed because the top entry was unique. Worth documenting as 'minimum rank among ties' behavior.

rerank_category_light writes ci = wilson_margin (line 1048-1049) — both fields now hold the same Wilson-margin value, but _rank_doc_to_entry computes its own 'ci' as ts_sigma*2*10 ignoring the stored ci. Stored ci is effectively dead. Either remove the write or stop computing on read.
test_credentials: Not required (public leaderboard + paper detail endpoints)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Dynamic rank refactor validated. Tests confirm: (1) /api/leaderboard ranks are sequential 1..N within page, (2) ts_score monotonically non-increasing in default sort, (3) custom sort (comparisons desc) produces sequential ranks ordered by comparisons, (4) offset pagination assigns ranks offset+1..offset+limit, (5) cursor pagination returns ranks starting at 1 on each page (frontend renumbers via useLeaderboardData.js loadMore startRank=leaderboard.length+1), (6) /api/papers/{id} returns current_rank matching leaderboard position via count_documents({ts_score:{$gt:X}})+1, (7) no 'rank_ts' field in responses. Visual frontend test showed ranks 1588-1601 continuous (no gap/restart).
rca of the issue: N/A — feature verification, no bugs found.
